originalTitle: Millions listen to Ethiopian star's song taking swipe at government
title: A Big Song About Being Friends in Ethiopia!
content:
Hello, Super Listeners!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter from Africa, here to tell you about something super cool happening in the wonderful country of Ethiopia! Imagine a superstar singer, like your favourite cartoon hero but for music, singing a song that everyone, everywhere, wants to hear! That's what's happening right now.

There's a very famous singer in Ethiopia named Teddy Afro. He's like a big music hero! He has a brand new song, and millions and millions of people are listening to it. That's like everyone in your whole town, and many, many more towns, listening all at once!

Did you know? Ethiopia is a beautiful country in Africa. It's known for its yummy coffee and ancient history. It even has its own special calendar!

Who is Teddy Afro?

So, who is Teddy Afro? He is the biggest musician in Ethiopia. This means lots and lots of people know him and love his songs. When he sings, his voice makes people want to dance and feel happy. He sings about many things, and his new song is very important.

Being a 'star' means you are very good at something and many people admire you. Teddy Afro is a music star! He works very hard to make his beautiful songs for everyone to enjoy. He makes music that has catchy beats and words that make you think.

Did you know? Music is like magic! It can make you feel happy, sad, or excited. It can also bring people together, no matter where they are from or what language they speak.

What is His New Song About?

Teddy Afro's new song has a very special message. It talks about 'unity'. What does unity mean? It means everyone being friends, working together, and helping each other. Imagine your classroom where everyone plays nicely and shares toys. That's unity!

The song gently reminds everyone that it's super important for people in a country to be kind and friendly to each other. When everyone is united, it's like a strong team working together to build something amazing. It makes the country stronger and happier for all the children and grown-ups too.

Instead of being cross or not getting along, Teddy Afro's song is like a big hug, asking everyone to come closer and be friends. He sings about making sure everyone feels like they belong and are part of one big family.

Millions Are Listening!

Can you imagine how many people 'millions' is? It's a super big number! If you count to a million, it would take you a very, very long time. So, Teddy Afro's song is being listened to by so many ears!

People are listening to his song on radios, on phones, and in their homes. When a song becomes this popular, it means its message is really important to many people. They like the music, and they like what he is saying in the song.

This shows how powerful music can be! A song can spread a message of friendship and working together far and wide, touching the hearts of many children and grown-ups. It helps everyone think about how they can be better friends and neighbours.

Did you know? When you listen to a song, it can make you feel things. A happy song can make you smile, and a calming song can help you relax. Teddy Afro's song is making many people think about being together.

Why is 'Unity' Important?

Unity is super important for every country, just like it's important for your school or your family. When people work together and are friends, they can do amazing things. They can build better schools, grow more food, and help each other when times are tough.

Think about a puzzle. If all the pieces fit together, you get a beautiful picture. But if some pieces are missing or don't fit, the picture isn't complete. A country is like that puzzle! When everyone is united, it makes a beautiful and strong country for everyone to live in.

Teddy Afro's song is a gentle reminder that being together is always better. It's about looking out for each other and remembering that we are all part of the same big family in our country. His music helps people remember to be kind and think of others.

Did you know? Working together is always more fun! If you and your friends clean up your toys together, it's faster and more fun than doing it all alone. That's the power of unity!

What Can We Learn?

From Teddy Afro's song, we can all learn a very important lesson: being kind, friendly, and working together makes everything better. Whether it's in your home, at your school, or in your country, unity helps everyone smile and be happy.

So next time you hear a song, listen closely to its message. Maybe it's telling you something important, just like Teddy Afro's new song is telling everyone in Ethiopia to be friends and work as one big team!

It's wonderful to see how music can bring so many people together and make them think about good things like friendship and helping each other. Keep singing and keep smiling!
summary: Teddy Afro, a very famous singer in Ethiopia, has a new song about everyone being friends and working together, and millions of people are listening to its important message of unity.
whyMatters: This matters because when a big star like Teddy Afro sings about everyone being friends and helping each other, it reminds many people to be kind and work together to make their country stronger and happier for everyone.

originalTitle: DR Congo accepts first set of deportees from the US
title: DR Congo accepts first set of deportees from the US
content: The Congolese government stresses those expelled from the US are only in the country temporarily.

originalTitle: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home
title: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home
content: For centuries the prized sculptures, central to national identity, have been kept outside Zimbabwe's borders.

originalTitle: North Korea launches ballistic missiles towards sea off its east coast
title: Rockets in the Sea! Far Away News for Kids!
content:
Hello Super Kids from Africa!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter, here to tell you about something happening far, far away. We love learning new things, don't we? Today, we are going to talk about a country called North Korea. It is very far from our beautiful Africa.

Big Rockets Go Splash!

Guess what happened? A country named North Korea sent some really big flying things into the sea. We call these big flying things "ballistic missiles." Can you say that? Ball-is-tic miss-iles! They are like super-fast rockets. They went "WHOOSH!" into the air and then "SPLASH!" into the water.

What Are Ballistic Missiles?

Imagine a very, very big firework that flies super high and super fast. That's a bit like a ballistic missile. But it's not for parties or fun. These are big tools that countries sometimes make. They are very serious and not toys for children to play with. Scientists build them very carefully. **Did you know?** The very first rockets were invented a long, long time ago in a country called China! They were not as big as today's rockets.

Where is North Korea?

North Korea is a country very, very far from us. If you look at a big map of the world, you would see Africa here, and North Korea is way over there, on the other side of a big ocean! It has its own people, schools, and homes, just like our countries in Africa. It is a country with mountains and rivers too.

Why Did They Send Them Into the Sea?

They sent these big rockets "towards the sea off its east coast." This means they shot them over a part of the sea that is empty. It's like throwing a pebble into a big pond where no one is swimming. They do this because it's a very big, empty space. It is a safe place for their tests. No houses or people are there. **Did you know?** The Earth is mostly covered in water! That's why we have so many big oceans and seas.

It Was a Test

The news tells us it was a "test." What does "test" mean? It's like when you try out a new game or a new recipe. You want to see if it works! This country was trying out their new big rockets. They want to see how fast they go and how far they can travel. They want to learn more about them.

Not the First Time This Year

This was not the first time North Korea did this this year. The news says it was the "seventh such test." That means they have done it six times before this year! It’s like when you try to learn to ride a bicycle. Sometimes you try, and try again, until you get it just right. But these are big rockets, not bicycles! **Did you know?** Some rockets are so powerful they can even take people all the way into space!

From the Sinpo Area

The big rockets were fired from a special place. It's called the "eastern Sinpo area." Imagine a special big launch pad, like a giant swing set for rockets! That's where they sent the big flying things up, up, and away. This area is near the sea, which is why the rockets splash into the water nearby.

Far Away from Africa

Now, my dear friends in Africa, remember this is happening very, very far away from us. Our homes, our schools, and our families are safe here. We live in peace. It's good to know what is happening in the world, even far away. But we should always remember that our part of the world is happy and peaceful. **Did you know?** Africa is a huge continent with many different countries, languages, and amazing animals like lions and elephants!

Why is This News Important?

When countries do things like launch big rockets, it makes other countries think. It's important for all countries to talk to each other and be good neighbours. Like how you talk to your friends and share your toys. When countries work together, we can all live in a happy, peaceful world. We want all countries to be friends. **Did you know?** There are over 190 countries in the world! Each one is special.

Let's Keep Learning and Being Kind

So, my young friends, that's the news from far away today. We learned about North Korea, big rockets, and the sea. Most importantly, we learned that peace and friendship are very, very important for everyone, everywhere. Let's always be kind to each other and keep learning amazing things about our big, beautiful world!
summary: A country called North Korea sent some big rockets into the sea to test them.
whyMatters: It's important for countries to be friends and not launch big rockets, so everyone can live in peace.

originalTitle: Iran war live: Tehran says no date set for US talks, Hormuz Strait closed
title: Iran war live: Tehran says no date set for US talks, Hormuz Strait closed
content: IRGC says the Strait of Hormuz will remain closed until the US stops blockading Iranian ports.

originalTitle: Trinidad and Tobago police uncover 56 bodies, mostly children, at cemetery
title: Trinidad and Tobago police uncover 56 bodies, mostly children, at cemetery
content: Police suspect the grim discovery in the city of Cumuto may be a case of 'unlawful disposal of unclaimed corpses'.

originalTitle: Strait of Hormuz closed again, Iran says, as ships attacked
title: Strait of Hormuz closed again, Iran says, as ships attacked
content: Iran is blaming a US blockade for the closure, saying it breaches the ceasefire reached between the two countries.

originalTitle: At least six killed in Kyiv as gunman opens fire and takes hostages
title: Brave Police Help People in a City Far, Far Away!
content:
Hello, Little Explorers!

Hello, amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you stories from all over the world. Today, we have some news from a place very far away from our beautiful Africa. It's a big city called Kyiv. Can you say Kyiv? It sounds like 'Kee-yev'. It's in a country called Ukraine, which is in Europe. So, it's a long, long way from home! Sometimes, things happen in the world that can make us feel a little bit sad or wonder why. But don't worry, my job is to tell you what happened in a simple way and to remind you that there are always heroes who help keep us safe.

What Happened in Kyiv?

Imagine a big shop, like a supermarket, where grown-ups go to buy yummy food, fresh fruits, and treats. One day, in this big supermarket in Kyiv, something not very kind happened. A person who was feeling very angry and sad inside did something that made many people scared. This person stopped other people from leaving the shop. They couldn't go home to their families. It was a scary time for the people inside, and for their families outside who were waiting for them. Everyone felt worried. **Did you know?** A supermarket is a very big shop where you can buy almost all your food and household items in one place!

Brave Police to the Rescue!

When something bad like this happens, we are so lucky to have very brave people who help. These people are the police! The police in Kyiv heard about what was happening. They knew that people needed help right away. So, what did the brave police do? They rushed to the supermarket! Their main job is to keep everyone safe and to make sure that people who do bad things are stopped. They are like real-life superheroes, but they wear uniforms and drive special cars. The police surrounded the supermarket. They talked to the person who was causing trouble. They worked very, very hard to make sure that the people inside the shop could come out safely. It took some time, and it was a difficult job, but the police are very well-trained to handle such situations.

Making Things Safe Again

The brave police kept working. They talked and planned. They did everything they could to make sure everyone was safe. Finally, after a lot of effort and bravery, the police were able to stop the person who was making trouble. Once the person was stopped, all the people who couldn't leave the shop were finally free! They could go back to their families. Imagine how happy and relieved they must have felt to hug their loved ones again. The police made sure the supermarket was safe for everyone once more. **Did you know?** Police officers train for a long time to learn how to help people and keep communities safe. They learn about talking to people, first aid, and even how to drive fast cars!

Sending Love and Kindness

Sadly, in this difficult event, some people got hurt very badly. And sadly, a few people are not with us anymore. This makes us feel very sad, and we send all our love and kind thoughts to their families and friends in Kyiv. Even though this happened far away, it reminds us to always be kind to each other. When we are kind, we make the world a happier and safer place for everyone. Sometimes people do bad things because they are very sad or confused inside, but there are always ways to get help and be kind instead. **Did you know?** There are many big cities all around the world, just like Kyiv! Each city has its own special buildings, parks, and people who live there.

Our Heroes Here in Africa

Remember, we also have amazing police and other helpers right here in our own communities in Africa! They work every day to keep us safe. If you ever feel scared or see something that doesn't look right, you can always tell a grown-up, like your parents, teachers, or a police officer. They are there to listen and to help. Being safe means knowing who to talk to when you need help, and knowing that there are good people all around you who care. So, even though we heard about something that happened far away, let's remember the brave police, send our love to those who are sad, and keep being kind and helpful to everyone around us. Because when we are kind, we make our corner of the world shine brightly! That's all for today from your kid-friendly news reporter. Stay safe and keep smiling!
summary: In the city of Kyiv, far away from Africa, brave police officers helped people escape a supermarket after a person caused trouble, making sure everyone was safe again, though sadly some people got hurt.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows us that even when bad things happen, there are always brave heroes like the police who help keep people safe, and it reminds us to be kind to each other and that help is always available.

originalTitle: Pope says 'tyrants' speech was not aimed at Trump
title: Pope says 'tyrants' speech was not aimed at Trump
content: The pontiff says his remarks have been misinterpreted after a spat with the US president.

originalTitle: Wild celebrations to verge of tears -  time running out for Spurs
title: Wild celebrations to verge of tears -  time running out for Spurs
content: Scenes of celebration turned to bitter disappointment as Tottenham's hopes of avoiding relegation from the Premier League were dealt a devastating blow against Brighton.

originalTitle: Protests and another costly loss - pressure mounts on Rosenior's Chelsea
title: Protests and another costly loss - pressure mounts on Rosenior's Chelsea
content: Chelsea head coach Liam Rosenior admits his side face a "mountain to climb" as their campaign unravels at a crucial juncture following defeat by Manchester United.

originalTitle: Carrick silences doubters as Man Utd close on Champions League
title: Carrick silences doubters as Man Utd close on Champions League
content: Manchester United beat Chelsea to move closer to a Champions League return and silence the growing Michael Carrick doubters.
